rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use valid AADL feature names and types from the analyzed model; do not introduce package-qualified identifiers.
Insert AGREE annex blocks within `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax in appropriate component type or implementation scopes.
generic_rules: Stateful equations must use initialized temporal recurrence patterns.
Branching logic in AGREE should use nested if-then-else expressions.
Component type contracts should relate visible inputs to outputs without referencing implementation-only paths.
AGREE contracts must use only visible identifiers and helper declarations from the same scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions use `assume "label": expression;` and guarantees use `guarantee "label": expression;`.
Equations are declared with `eq name : type = expression;` and constants with `const name : type = value;`.
Assignments in implementation scope use `assign output = expression;`.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
Do not reference subcomponent ports or implementation-only paths in component type annexes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or AADL property names as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
